canonical_definition: The ability to recognize and reason about an underlying concept across different visual representations, even when its form, structure, or manifestation varies significantly.

definitions.text: The ability to induce and represent a coherent, generalizable rule that explains a set of visual examples, enabling discrimination between two groups of diagrams.

relationships.evidence: “Bongard problems (BP), introduced in 1970 by Mikhail Bongard (Bongard & Hawkins, 1970), are visual puzzles that test for capabilities of pattern recognition, concept formation, and abstraction.”
